summary: Install-time Windows DLL dropper with scheduled-task persistence and self-erasure
details: On `npm install`, the package's `install` lifecycle script (lib/chart-loader.js) fetches an XOR-encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, decrypts it with single-byte XOR key 'B', base64-decodes the result to a Windows PE/DLL (verified by MZ header), writes it to %APPDATA%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll under a Microsoft-mimicking path, and executes it via `spawn('rundll32.exe', [dllPath, 'Run'], { detached: true })`. The script then modifies a scheduled task named `WindowsUpdateService` via `schtasks /change` for persistence, and spawns a detached node process that after a 3-second delay deletes the package directory and edits the parent project's package.json to remove the `analysis-chart` dependency entry — concealing the install footprint from the installer. The index.js statistics API is decoy cover; the install-time dropper is the package's real function. The publisher identity (`Analysis-Chart` org, `elena@analysis-chart.io`) appears fabricated to lend legitimacy to the payload host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
